values.name: User

values.name: user
values.doc: Indicates the operation is initiated by a user.

values.name: system
values.doc: Indicates the operation is initiated by a system.

values.name: user,system
values.doc: Indicates the operation is initiated by a user or system.

enums.doc: The intended executor of the operation; as in Resource Based Access Control (RBAC) and audit logs UX. Default value is "user,system"

enums.name: ActionType
enums.doc: Extensible enum. Indicates the action type. "Internal" refers to actions that are for internal only APIs.

enums.name: DiscountEntityType
enums.doc: This defines whether the entity being created is primary or affiliate. Supported values: primary, affiliate. Validation: Required, must match one of the 2 values.

enums.name: DiscountProvisioningState
enums.doc: Provisioning states of Discount.

enums.name: DiscountStatus
enums.doc: Represents the current status of the discount.

enums.name: DiscountAppliedScopeType
enums.doc: List of applied scopes supported for discounts.

enums.name: DiscountType
enums.doc: Defines the type of discount. Supported values are ProductFamily, Product, Sku, CustomPrice, and CustomPriceMultiCurrency.

enums.name: ApplyDiscountOn
enums.doc: The customer action on which the discount is applied. Supported values are Purchase, Consume, and Renew. Validation: Required, one of supported values.

enums.name: DiscountCombinationRule
enums.doc: The discount combination rule when there are multiple applicable custom prices. Validation: Required. Supported values are Stackable and BestOf.

enums.name: PricingPolicy
enums.doc: Supported values: Protected, Locked

enums.name: DiscountRuleType
enums.doc: The type of the priceable node pricing rule. Validation: Required. Supported values are fixedPriceLock, fixedListPrice, and priceCeiling.

enums.name: ManagedServiceIdentityType
enums.doc: Type of managed service identity (where both SystemAssigned and UserAssigned types are allowed).

enums.name: SkuTier
enums.doc: This field is required to be implemented by the Resource Provider if the service has more than one tier, but is not required on a PUT.

enums.name: ResourceProvisioningState
enums.doc: The provisioning state of a resource type.

enums.name: BillingBenefitsPaymentStatus
enums.doc: Describes whether the payment is completed, failed, cancelled or scheduled in the future.

enums.name: BillingBenefitsReservedResourceType
enums.doc: The type of the resource that is being reserved.

enums.name: BillingBenefitsInstanceFlexibility
enums.doc: Turning this on will apply the reservation discount to other VMs in the same VM size group.

enums.name: Versions
enums.doc: The available API versions.

baseModel.name: ProxyResource

baseModel.name: Resource
baseModel.doc: Common fields that are returned in the response for all Azure Resource Manager resources

properties.name: id

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The name of the resource

properties.name: type
properties.doc: The type of the resource. E.g. "Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines" or "Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts"

properties.name: systemData
properties.doc: Azure Resource Manager metadata containing createdBy and modifiedBy information.

properties.name: lastModifiedBy

properties.name: lastModifiedByType

properties.name: lastModifiedAt
properties.doc: The timestamp of resource last modification (UTC)

models.name: OperationListResult
models.doc: A list of REST API operations supported by an Azure Resource Provider. It contains an URL link to get the next set of results.

properties.name: value
properties.doc: The Operation items on this page

valueType.name: Operation
valueType.doc: Details of a REST API operation, returned from the Resource Provider Operations API

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The name of the operation, as per Resource-Based Access Control (RBAC). Examples: "Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/write", "Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/capture/action"

properties.name: isDataAction
properties.doc: Whether the operation applies to data-plane. This is "true" for data-plane operations and "false" for Azure Resource Manager/control-plane operations.

properties.name: display
properties.doc: Localized display information for this particular operation.

type.name: OperationDisplay
type.doc: Localized display information for and operation.

properties.name: provider
properties.doc: The localized friendly form of the resource provider name, e.g. "Microsoft Monitoring Insights" or "Microsoft Compute".

properties.name: resource
properties.doc: The localized friendly name of the resource type related to this operation. E.g. "Virtual Machines" or "Job Schedule Collections".

properties.name: operation
properties.doc: The concise, localized friendly name for the operation; suitable for dropdowns. E.g. "Create or Update Virtual Machine", "Restart Virtual Machine".

properties.name: description
properties.doc: The short, localized friendly description of the operation; suitable for tool tips and detailed views.

properties.name: origin
properties.doc: The intended executor of the operation; as in Resource Based Access Control (RBAC) and audit logs UX. Default value is "user,system"

properties.name: actionType
properties.doc: Extensible enum. Indicates the action type. "Internal" refers to actions that are for internal only APIs.

properties.name: nextLink
properties.doc: The link to the next page of items

baseModel.name: TrackedResource
baseModel.doc: The resource model definition for an Azure Resource Manager tracked top level resource which has 'tags' and a 'location'

properties.name: tags
properties.doc: Resource tags.

properties.name: location
properties.doc: The geo-location where the resource lives

properties.name: properties
properties.doc: Discount properties

discriminatorProperty.name: entityType
discriminatorProperty.doc: This defines whether the entity being created is primary or affiliate. Supported values: primary, affiliate. Validation: Required, must match one of the 2 values.

properties.name: productCode
properties.doc: This is the catalog UPN for the product.

properties.name: startAt
properties.doc: Start date of the discount. Value is the date the discount started or will start in the future.

properties.name: systemId
properties.doc: This is the globally unique identifier of the Discount which will not change for the lifetime of the Discount.

properties.name: provisioningState
properties.doc: The state of the resource. Supported values are Pending, Failed, Succeeded, Canceled.

properties.name: billingAccountResourceId
properties.doc: Billing account resource id where the discount metadata is present.

properties.name: billingProfileResourceId
properties.doc: Billing profile resource id where the discount is scoped to.

properties.name: customerResourceId
properties.doc: Customer resource id where the discount is scoped to.

properties.name: displayName
properties.doc: This defines a user friendly display name for the discount.

properties.name: status
properties.doc: Represents the current status of the discount.

properties.name: benefitResourceId
properties.doc: Fully-qualified identifier of the benefit under applicable benefit list.

properties.name: appliedScopeType
properties.doc: List of applied scopes supported for discounts.

properties.name: primaryResourceId
properties.doc: This will be present in the response if the primary has a resource ID

properties.name: endAt
properties.doc: End date of the discount. No duration will be supported. Allowed value is any date greater than or equal to startDate.

properties.name: entityType
properties.doc: This defines whether the entity being created is primary or affiliate. Supported values: primary, affiliate. Validation: Required, must match one of the 2 values.

properties.name: discountTypeProperties
properties.doc: This defines the conditions for a given discount type.

type.name: DiscountTypeProperties
type.doc: This defines the conditions for a given discount type.

discriminatorProperty.name: discountType
discriminatorProperty.doc: Defines the type of discount. Supported values are ProductFamily, Product, Sku, CustomPrice, and CustomPriceMultiCurrency.

properties.name: applyDiscountOn
properties.doc: The customer action on which the discount is applied. Supported values are Purchase, Consume, and Renew. Validation: Required, one of supported values.

properties.name: discountPercentage
properties.doc: Discount percentage provided for the customer. Validation: Required unless this is a price rule.

properties.name: discountCombinationRule
properties.doc: The discount combination rule when there are multiple applicable custom prices. Validation: Required. Supported values are Stackable and BestOf.

properties.name: priceGuaranteeProperties
properties.doc: Set only in price guarantee scenario.

type.name: PriceGuaranteeProperties
type.doc: Set only in price guarantee scenario.

properties.name: pricingPolicy
properties.doc: Supported values: Protected, Locked

properties.name: priceGuaranteeDate
properties.doc: The date on which prices are to be used for guarantee calculation. Validation: expected to be 00 hours, Format: 2024-09-30T00:00:00Z. Must be in UTC.

properties.name: conditions
properties.doc: Array of conditions for the discount. Validation: Optional. Maximum length is 1000.

properties.name: value
properties.doc: These items are open-ended strings.

ProductFamily.name: DiscountTypeProductFamily
ProductFamily.doc: Discount type properties including product family name

properties.name: productFamilyName
properties.doc: Product family for which the discount is given. Validation: Optional

properties.name: discountType
properties.doc: Defines the type of discount. Supported values are ProductFamily, Product, Sku, CustomPrice, and CustomPriceMultiCurrency.

Product.name: DiscountTypeProduct
Product.doc: Discount type properties including product family name and product id.

properties.name: productFamilyName
properties.doc: Product family for which the discount is given. Validation: Optional

properties.name: productId
properties.doc: Product ID for which the discount is given. Validation: Optional. No specific format, example: DZH318Z09V6F

properties.name: discountType
properties.doc: Defines the type of discount. Supported values are ProductFamily, Product, Sku, CustomPrice, and CustomPriceMultiCurrency.

Sku.name: DiscountTypeProductSku
Sku.doc: Discount type properties including product family name, product id, and sku id.

properties.name: productFamilyName
properties.doc: Product family for which the discount is given. Validation: Optional

properties.name: productId
properties.doc: Product ID for which the discount is given. Validation: Optional. No specific format, example: DZH318Z09V6F

properties.name: skuId
properties.doc: ResourceSku for the given discount. Validation: Optional.

properties.name: discountType
properties.doc: Defines the type of discount. Supported values are ProductFamily, Product, Sku, CustomPrice, and CustomPriceMultiCurrency.

CustomPrice.name: DiscountTypeCustomPrice
CustomPrice.doc: Discount type properties including product family name, product id, sku, and custom price properties. Allows a single entry in marketSetPrices.

discriminatorProperty.name: discountType
discriminatorProperty.doc: Defines the type of discount. Supported values are ProductFamily, Product, Sku, CustomPrice, and CustomPriceMultiCurrency.

properties.name: productFamilyName
properties.doc: Product family for which the discount is given. Validation: Optional

properties.name: productId
properties.doc: Product ID for which the discount is given. Validation: Optional. No specific format, example: DZH318Z09V6F

properties.name: skuId
properties.doc: ResourceSku for the given discount. Validation: Optional.

properties.name: customPriceProperties
properties.doc: Custom price properties for a given discount.

type.name: CustomPriceProperties
type.doc: Custom price properties for a given discount.

properties.name: ruleType
properties.doc: The type of the priceable node pricing rule. Validation: Required. Supported values are fixedPriceLock, fixedListPrice, and priceCeiling.

properties.name: catalogId
properties.doc: The catalog instance where the priceable node lives. Validation: Required. No defined format, will vary per team.

properties.name: catalogClaims
properties.doc: The set of BigCat claims. Validation: Required. Must contain AgreementType, NationalCloud, and PricingAudience claims. Additionally requires AccessPass claim when creating custom price with action == consume on the pricing instructions.

properties.name: termUnits
properties.doc: The term units for the priceable node. Validation: Optional, Maximum length 128 characters. Must be present if and only if the availability derived by market, product, sku, and claims has terms.

properties.name: billingPeriod
properties.doc: The billing period of the priceable node. Validation: Optional, Maximum length 128 characters. Only allowed if the availability derived by market, product, sku, and claims has terms and at least one of those terms has a billing period. When specified, termUnits must be specified.

properties.name: meterType
properties.doc: Must be present if the market, product, sku, and claims, and optional term information resolves to multiple availabilities that only differ by meter type. Validation: Maximum length 128 characters.

properties.name: marketSetPrices
properties.doc: The set of market set prices of the priceable node. Validation: Required. Must contain at least one element.

properties.name: value
properties.doc: The locked price for the priceable node. Validation: Required. Must be greater than or equal to 0. If the case of billing plans. This represents the price for each cycle charge.

properties.name: currency
properties.doc: The currency of the locked price value. Validation: Required. Must be a valid ISO 4217 3-letter currency code.

CustomPriceMultiCurrency.name: DiscountTypeCustomPriceMultiCurrency
CustomPriceMultiCurrency.doc: Discount type properties including product family name, product id, sku, and custom price properties. Allows multiple entries in marketSetPrices.

properties.name: discountType
properties.doc: Defines the type of discount. Supported values are ProductFamily, Product, Sku, CustomPrice, and CustomPriceMultiCurrency.

properties.name: endAt
properties.doc: End date of the discount. No duration will be supported. Allowed value is any date greater than or equal to startDate.

properties.name: entityType
properties.doc: This defines whether the entity being created is primary or affiliate. Supported values: primary, affiliate. Validation: Required, must match one of the 2 values.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: Name of the discount

properties.name: managedBy
properties.doc: The fully qualified resource ID of the resource that manages this resource. Indicates if this resource is managed by another Azure resource. If this is present, complete mode deployment will not delete the resource if it is removed from the template since it is managed by another resource.

properties.name: kind
properties.doc: Metadata used by portal/tooling/etc to render different UX experiences for resources of the same type. E.g. ApiApps are a kind of Microsoft.Web/sites type.  If supported, the resource provider must validate and persist this value.

properties.name: etag
properties.doc: The etag field is *not* required. If it is provided in the response body, it must also be provided as a header per the normal etag convention.  Entity tags are used for comparing two or more entities from the same requested resource. HTTP/1.1 uses entity tags in the etag (section 14.19), If-Match (section 14.24), If-None-Match (section 14.26), and If-Range (section 14.27) header fields.

properties.name: identity
properties.doc: Managed service identity (system assigned and/or user assigned identities)

type.name: ManagedServiceIdentity
type.doc: Managed service identity (system assigned and/or user assigned identities)

properties.name: principalId
properties.doc: The service principal ID of the system assigned identity. This property will only be provided for a system assigned identity.

properties.name: tenantId
properties.doc: The tenant ID of the system assigned identity. This property will only be provided for a system assigned identity.

properties.name: type
properties.doc: Type of managed service identity (where both SystemAssigned and UserAssigned types are allowed).

properties.name: userAssignedIdentities
properties.doc: The set of user assigned identities associated with the resource. The userAssignedIdentities dictionary keys will be ARM resource ids in the form: '/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.ManagedIdentity/userAssignedIdentities/{identityName}. The dictionary values can be empty objects ({}) in requests.

properties.name: principalId
properties.doc: The principal ID of the assigned identity.

properties.name: clientId
properties.doc: The client ID of the assigned identity.

properties.name: sku
properties.doc: The resource model definition representing SKU

type.name: Sku
type.doc: The resource model definition representing SKU

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The name of the SKU. E.g. P3. It is typically a letter+number code

properties.name: tier
properties.doc: This field is required to be implemented by the Resource Provider if the service has more than one tier, but is not required on a PUT.

properties.name: size
properties.doc: The SKU size. When the name field is the combination of tier and some other value, this would be the standalone code.

properties.name: family
properties.doc: If the service has different generations of hardware, for the same SKU, then that can be captured here.

properties.name: capacity
properties.doc: If the SKU supports scale out/in then the capacity integer should be included. If scale out/in is not possible for the resource this may be omitted.

properties.name: plan
properties.doc: Plan for the resource.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: A user defined name of the 3rd Party Artifact that is being procured.

properties.name: publisher
properties.doc: The publisher of the 3rd Party Artifact that is being bought. E.g. NewRelic

properties.name: product
properties.doc: The 3rd Party artifact that is being procured. E.g. NewRelic. Product maps to the OfferID specified for the artifact at the time of Data Market onboarding.

properties.name: promotionCode
properties.doc: A publisher provided promotion code as provisioned in Data Market for the said product/artifact.

properties.name: version
properties.doc: The version of the desired product/artifact.

parameters.name: $filter
parameters.doc: May be used to filter by reservation properties. The filter supports 'eq', 'or', and 'and'. It does not currently support 'ne', 'gt', 'le', 'ge', or 'not'. Reservation properties include sku/name, properties/{appliedScopeType, archived, displayName, displayProvisioningState, effectiveDateTime, expiryDate, provisioningState, quantity, renew, reservedResourceType, term, userFriendlyAppliedScopeType, userFriendlyRenewState}

parameters.name: $orderby
parameters.doc: May be used to sort order by reservation properties.

parameters.doc: To indicate whether to refresh the roll up counts of the savings plans group by provisioning states

parameters.doc: The number of savings plans to skip from the list before returning results

parameters.name: take
parameters.doc: To number of savings plans to return
